hard.next_step: 50% Confidence 6th Jun- evaluation has slowed while ME situation improves May 11- no update- KIV 4th May- sent 1:1 email to Wun Wai Apr 27 Not able to re-ngage. Feedback from partner is the same. Changi became silent as working internally on RFP (expected by mid-year).
hard.ais_why: Rep positions superior AI capabilities as the CFO-level win theme (4 Feb 2026), but no named Changi stakeholder is quoted using AI language and the buyer has gone silent; product scope is mixed traditional plus AI with no Merlin/Agentic module attached.

north_star_verdict.headline: Buyer has been silent since the 4 Feb 2026 positive meeting; the promised mid-year RFP never landed, evaluation has slowed, the budget was cut 750k to 550k, so this Qualified deal needs a full re-engagement before any 25 Sep close is credible.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 29 Nov 2024, qualified 16 Sep 2025, 81 days to close (25 Sep 2026) from the 6 Jul 2026 sweep. Roughly 10 months in Qualified with no submission-date field populated and no formal RFP, shortlist, or evaluation started. Field history shows the amount cut 750k to 550k (6 Jun 2026), close pulled in 20 Nov 2026 to 25 Sep 2026 (3 Mar 2026), and the owner changed to Carl Kimball (6 Jun 2026). The last genuine two-way buyer exchange was Nursakina Noorani's 13 Mar 2026 email reply; every buyer touch since is rep-side outbound (Could Not Connect on 26/29 May, Horizon and AI World Tour email invites) with no reply, and Next Step 6 Jun 2026 reports evaluation slowed. Cold well beyond 60 days forces Off Track; the deal is not dead (an RFP is still expected) but momentum must be rebuilt.
evidence: Next Step 6 Jun 2026: evaluation has slowed while ME (macro-environment) situation improves; confidence marked 50%
Next Step 27 Apr 2026: Not able to re-engage. Feedback from partner is the same. Changi became silent as working internally on RFP (expected by mid-year)
Next Step 13 Apr 2026: working internally on RFP to be delivered by June. Became silent. KIV

Next Step 4 Feb 2026: Positive meeting with strong possibility to be part of short list (RFP in second half of 2026). Action to engage with CFO to get executive buying based on superior AI capabilities. Still need to work on mitigating previous experience
Next Step 16 Sep 2024: Key stakeholder Mun Wai Mak is not favoring Zycus, but is open to meet and hear proposition
Field history 6 Jun 2026: Amount 750000 to 550000, Owner Guillaume Pasquet to Carl Kimball (by Carl Kimball)

metrics.narrative: No quantified value case exists anywhere in Salesforce or the Next Step history, and with zero Avoma calls there is no buyer-stated number to mine. The 4 Feb 2026 Next Step frames the pitch around superior AI capabilities and mitigating the previous experience, but no cost, cycle-time, headcount, savings, or compliance figure has ever been articulated by a Changi stakeholder. The opportunity is still qualitative and tied to the broader S4 HANA migration narrative. Building a quantified AI-led sourcing and contract-intelligence ROI with Mak Mun Wai (Vice President Finance) is the move that would give the CFO a concrete reason to prioritise the evaluation.

economic_buyer.narrative: Mak Mun Wai, Vice President Finance, is the identified economic buyer: he sits on the OpportunityContactRole and is the named CFO target across the Next Step history and nearly every 2026 outbound touch. Authority is clear but sponsorship is not: the 16 Sep 2024 note states he is not favouring Zycus due to a past project, and the 4 Feb 2026 action is still to engage the CFO to get executive buying, meaning he has not been won. Recent contact is entirely one-way (1:1 email 27 Apr and 4 May 2026, Horizon invite 15 Jun 2026, outbound calls 26/29 May marked Could Not Connect), so there is no live two-way EB engagement. Converting him from cautious gatekeeper to sponsor, using the STE and NTUC reference wins to rebuild trust, is the central EB task.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Mak Mun Wai, Vice President Finance

decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria are documented because the RFP has not been released; the buyer is repeatedly described as working internally on it (13 and 27 Apr 2026). The only directional signals are the rep-asserted emphasis on superior AI capabilities (4 Feb 2026) and the 2 Dec 2024 note that SIs were invited to share Gen AI use cases, suggesting AI-led innovation will feature in the eventual criteria. Nothing is buyer-confirmed. Securing an early view of the draft RFP criteria from KPMG or Accenture, and shaping the AI requirements before they are frozen, is the way to close this gap.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 4 Feb 2026, 13 Apr 2026, 27 Apr 2026, 2 Dec 2024

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a partner-influenced, RFP-led selection tied to its S4 HANA ERP migration: Accenture confirmed CAG should go to market in June/July as the SAP migration is on track (3 Mar 2026) and KPMG has repeatedly reconfirmed the evaluation timing (16 Sep 2024). Procurement (Ng Ching, Nursakina Noorani, Chua Lay Ling), Finance (Mak Mun Wai) and Corporate IT (Yuh Khee Leong) all appear in the org picture, indicating a multi-function committee. What is unknown is the internal approval chain and who owns the RFP; no Salesforce process field or call confirms it, and the RFP promised by mid-year has slipped. Getting the RFP timeline and decision owners in writing from the partners is the way to firm this up.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal, procurement channel, InfoSec) have surfaced, which is expected for a Qualified deal with no RFP yet issued. No Current Contract Expiration is populated, so there is no incumbent-renewal forcing function. As a Singapore aviation enterprise mid-way through an S4 HANA migration, Changi will almost certainly require an InfoSec review and ERP-integration scoping once a vendor is selected, but none of that is visible yet. Visibility only comes after the RFP lands and a procurement channel is named.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is inferred rather than buyer-stated: procurement transformation is being pulled along by the S4 HANA ERP migration (24 Nov 2024, main focus is S4H migration; 3 Mar 2026, SAP migration now on track), and the 2 Dec 2024 invitation to SIs to share Gen AI use cases signals appetite to modernise sourcing with AI. A distinct, deal-shaping element is the reputational overhang from a past Zycus project (12 Sep 2024, not well positioned because of past project) that colours the whole evaluation. No pain owner has quantified the operational cost of the status quo. A discovery session with procurement and IT to name and size the specific pain Zycus AI addresses is the move.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 24 Nov 2024, 3 Mar 2026, 2 Dec 2024, 12 Sep 2024

champion.narrative: There is no champion. The economic buyer, Mak Mun Wai, is cautious and not favouring Zycus, so he cannot carry the deal internally. Procurement leaders (Ng Ching, Associate General Manager; Nursakina Noorani, Assistant Manager; Chua Lay Ling, Associate Director) and Corporate IT (Yuh Khee Leong, Senior Vice President) are plausible champion candidates who engaged around the Feb 2026 meetings, but none shows advocacy behaviour: no internal selling, no RFP-criteria access, no pushing back on rivals for us. The buyer going silent since spring with only Could Not Connect outbound attempts underlines the single-threaded, championless state. Cultivating one senior procurement or IT sponsor is the highest-priority relationship move.

competition.narrative: Four rivals are named in Competitors__c: SAP Ariba, GEP Worldwide, iValua and Coupa. SAP Ariba is the strongest current threat because Changi's S4 HANA migration is on track (Accenture, 3 Mar 2026), giving Ariba a native-ERP integration argument the others lack. No call quotes a buyer preference for any vendor, so the ranking rests on the ERP-integration logic plus the standing field, not on a stated buyer lean. Zycus's real drag is the reputational overhang from a past project rather than a rival's lead; the wedge is agentic AI differentiation and the STE and NTUC reference wins, which must land with the CFO before the RFP is written.

deal_movement.summary: The pulse is dark. The last genuine two-way buyer exchange was Nursakina Noorani's 13 Mar 2026 email reply; since then it is all rep-side outbound. The budget was cut 750k to 550k and the deal was reassigned to Carl Kimball on 6 Jun 2026, and the close date was pulled in from 20 Nov to 25 Sep on 3 Mar 2026, all while the promised mid-year RFP failed to appear.

Last two-way buyer exchange: Nursakina Noorani email reply on the roundtable thread

competitive_position.summary: Recency-weighted, SAP Ariba is the single strongest current threat: Changi's S4 HANA migration is on track (Accenture, 3 Mar 2026), giving Ariba a native-ERP integration advantage, and no buyer has stated a preference for anyone else. GEP Worldwide, iValua and Coupa remain on the standing competitor field from deal creation with no fresh buyer signal, so they rank below Ariba as watch items. No competitor is buyer-preferred on the evidence; Zycus's real drag is the reputational overhang from a past project, not a named rival's lead. The wedge is agentic AI differentiation plus STE and NTUC references to rebuild trust before the RFP is written.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is inferred from the migration context, not buyer-confirmed criteria. Changi wants procurement modernisation aligned to its S4 HANA programme and AI-led innovation, which plays to Zycus strengths, but the past-project experience leaves Zycus exposed on trust until references land.

best_practice_check.summary: This is an early-stage, cold, championless deal whose main threat is buyer inertia plus SAP Ariba's native-ERP pull. The win levers are re-opening a two-way conversation, building a first champion, and pre-shaping the RFP before it is frozen.
flags: Break the silence with a value-first re-engagement to Mak Mun Wai before the RFP is frozen; four months of one-way outbound has produced no reply.
Develop a first champion in procurement (Ng Ching or Nursakina Noorani), who engaged in Feb 2026, since the CFO is a cautious gatekeeper who will not carry Zycus internally.
Neutralise the past-project trust gap early with STE and NTUC reference proof, or SAP Ariba's on-track S4 HANA integration story wins the RFP by default.

champion_strength.summary: No champion. The economic buyer, Mak Mun Wai (Vice President Finance), is cautious and championing no one internally, and no procurement or IT contact has shown advocacy behaviour. Ng Ching (Associate General Manager) and Nursakina Noorani (Assistant Manager, Procurement) are the most plausible candidates to develop, but both must be re-engaged from a multi-month silence first.
champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole; SF Next_Step__c 4 Feb 2026, 16 Sep 2024

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus is positioning superior AI capabilities as the CFO-level win theme (rep note 4 Feb 2026), but no Changi stakeholder has been quoted using AI language and the opp's product list carries no Merlin/Agentic module, so the agentic story is rep-asserted and under-landed with the buyer.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 5.0 / Moderate. The rep names superior AI capabilities as the engagement strategy and the 2 Dec 2024 note shows the buyer invited SIs to share Gen AI use cases, but no named Changi stakeholder is on record wanting AI, so the appetite is inferred and unconfirmed rather than demonstrated.

scope_change.detail: Carl Kimball cut the amount from 750k to 550k on 6 Jun 2026 while marking the evaluation slowed at 50% confidence; a defensive budget reduction rather than a documented module drop (the full S2P product list is unchanged).

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A cold, championless Qualified deal: the buyer has been silent since 13 Mar 2026, the budget was cut to 550k, the CFO is not favouring Zycus over a past-project overhang, and the promised RFP never landed, so the only path is a full re-engagement before the 25 Sep close.

win_position.text: Weak position: economic buyer Mak Mun Wai (Vice President Finance) is a cautious gatekeeper not favouring Zycus over a past project, and there is no champion to carry the deal (SF Next_Step__c 16 Sep 2024).
SAP Ariba holds a native-ERP integration edge now that Changi's S4 HANA migration is on track (Accenture, 3 Mar 2026), and no buyer has stated any preference for Zycus.
The budget was cut from 750k to 550k on 6 Jun 2026 as the evaluation slowed, a defensive signal that the deal is de-prioritising.

deal_momentum.text: The last two-way buyer exchange was Nursakina Noorani's 13 Mar 2026 email; every touch since is unanswered outbound including Could Not Connect calls on 26/29 May 2026.
The RFP promised by mid-year never appeared and Next Step 6 Jun 2026 reports evaluation has slowed at 50% confidence.

customer_commitment.text: No customer-owned dates, no RFP scope shared, and no next meeting requested; the buyer owes the RFP timeline it went silent on (13 Apr 2026).
The CFO target has not accepted any of the Horizon or AI World Tour invitations sent between April and June 2026.

deal_risk.text: Do-nothing inertia is the primary threat: Changi keeps managing procurement inside its ERP programme and may never issue a standalone S2P RFP, a no-decision risk more than a loss to a rival.
The past-project reputational overhang (12 Sep 2024) actively suppresses executive sponsorship until reference proof lands.

champion_strength.evidence: No champion; EB is a cautious gatekeeper not favouring Zycus (SF Next_Step__c 16 Sep 2024)

engagement.evidence: Cold since 13 Mar 2026; all outbound unanswered, evaluation slowed (Next Step 6 Jun 2026)

customer_preference.evidence: Key stakeholder Mun Wai Mak is not favouring Zycus due to a past project, only open to hear the proposition (SF Next_Step__c 16 Sep 2024); no buyer has praised Zycus or asked for Zycus-specific next steps.

business_case.evidence: No ROI, savings, or automation figure has ever been quantified by a Changi stakeholder; the AI value theme is entirely rep-asserted.

critical_signals.text: The last real two-way contact was Nursakina Noorani's 13 Mar 2026 email; every touch since is unanswered outbound, so the deal has effectively gone dark.
The budget was cut from 750k to 550k on 6 Jun 2026 as the evaluation slowed, a defensive signal the buyer is de-prioritising.
SAP Ariba is the front-runner by default: Changi's S4 HANA migration is on track (Accenture, 3 Mar 2026), handing Ariba a native-ERP integration edge no buyer has weighed Zycus against.
Economic buyer Mak Mun Wai (Vice President Finance) remains a cautious gatekeeper we have never converted, and no champion has emerged across procurement or IT.
